quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Plant Cell Labeling & Membrane Function
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Plant Cell Organelles, Cell Membrane Selectivity, Organelle Identification

questions.question: When a red stain is added to a culture containing both living and dead cells, only the dead cells take up the stain. Which structure prevents the stain from entering the living cells?
options: Nucleus, which regulates what enters and leaves the cell
Cytoplasm, which acts as a barrier to harmful substances
Cell membrane, which controls the passage of substances into and out of the cell
Cell wall, which blocks stains from penetrating the cell
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable and controls what enters and leaves the cell. In living cells, it actively prevents stains from entering. Dead cells have damaged membranes, allowing the stain to enter freely.

questions.question: What does the part labelled A represent?
questions.explanation: The nucleolus is a dense region inside the nucleus where ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is synthesized and ribosome subunits are assembled.

questions.question: What does the part labelled B represent?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains the cell's genetic material (DNA) and controls cellular activities such as growth, metabolism, and reproduction.

questions.question: What does the part labelled C represent?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are small structures that synthesize proteins by translating messenger RNA. They can be free in the cytoplasm or attached to rough ER.

questions.question: What does the part labelled D represent?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and carry out photosynthesis, converting light energy into chemical energy (glucose). They are found only in plant cells and some algae.

questions.question: What does the part labelled E represent?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, producing ATP through cellular respiration. They have their own DNA and double membranes.

questions.question: What does the part labelled F represent?
questions.explanation: Vacuoles store nutrients, water, and waste. Plant cells have a large central vacuole that maintains turgor pressure and stores various substances.

questions.question: What does the part labelled G represent?
questions.explanation: The cell wall is a rigid outer layer composed primarily of cellulose that provides structural support and protection to plant cells.

questions.question: What does the part labelled H represent?
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids for transport to other parts of the cell or for secretion.

questions.question: What does the part labelled I represent?
questions.explanation: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (Smooth ER) lacks ribosomes and is involved in lipid synthesis, detoxification, and calcium storage.

questions.question: What does the part labelled J represent?
questions.explanation: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (Rough ER) has ribosomes attached to its surface and is involved in protein synthesis and processing.

questions.question: What does the part labelled K represent?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane (plasma membrane) is a selectively permeable barrier that controls the movement of substances in and out of the cell.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can identify plant cell organelles with precision.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review a few organelle structures and their functions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between similar-looking organelles.
scoreMessages.0: Please review plant cell structure and organelle identification.
